methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $113M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Cresilon is a Series B biotech company that has raised $113M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private companies in the Biotech & Health sector at the same stage, alongside 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two methods triangulate valuation. Stage-progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $113M total raised proxy, implying $215M. Peer-median valuation across five same-stage biotech firms—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $810M, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring is not applicable; while the three public peers trade at a median 5.8x price-to-sales, Cresilon's revenue is not on file, preventing P/S multiple application.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $182M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $512M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage dynamics versus peer scaling. The stage-progression method, grounded in $113M raised, anchors the lower bound. Peer median valuation of $810M sits above the triangulated median, suggesting either Cresilon's stage-specific fundamentals lag the peer cohort or that peer set includes outliers. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, clinical stage/risk, and capital efficiency relative to named peers.
